en:
It is only in recent years that man has begun to understand how long the earth has existed.
For centuries, people assumed that human beings had always been present, unchanged since the dawn of time.
Archaeologists working in Africa and Asia have unearthed fossil remains dating back millions of years.
These discoveries, buried under layers of rock, have forced us to revise our picture of human origins.
The evidence is scarce, yet each new find may alter what we think we know.
Tools found beside the bones suggest that early man was already capable of making simple implements.
Buried for ages, the fossils were preserved by chance in conditions that rarely occur.
Finding fossil man remains one of the greatest challenges facing modern science.
cn:
直到近年，人类才开始理解地球存在了多久。
数个世纪以来，人们一直以为人类自古就存在，自创世以来未曾改变。
在非洲和亚洲工作的考古学家发掘出了可追溯至数百万年前的化石遗骸。
这些埋在岩层之下的发现，迫使我们修正对人类起源的认识。
证据虽稀少，每一次新发现却都可能改变我们的既有观念。
骨骼旁发现的工具表明，早期人类已能制造简单器具。
化石埋藏了数千年，碰巧保存在极为罕见的条件下。
发现化石人仍是现代科学面临的最大挑战之一。

en:
Spiders are not insects, as many people think, but small creatures belonging to a class of their own.
A single spider living in your garden may destroy over two thousand insects in a season.
Without spiders, pests that damage crops would multiply beyond control.
Scientists who study spider silk hope to develop materials stronger than steel.
The web, which looks delicate, is in fact one of nature's most remarkable structures.
We need not love spiders, but we should learn to tolerate them.
Next time you see one in the corner, spare it — for the balance of nature depends on such humble allies.
Removing them would upset an ecological system that took millions of years to evolve.
cn:
蜘蛛并非昆虫——许多人这样以为——而是属于另一纲类的小生物。
住在你花园里的一只蜘蛛，一个季节就能消灭两千多只昆虫。
没有蜘蛛，危害庄稼的害虫便会失控繁殖。
研究蜘蛛丝的科学家希望开发出比钢铁更坚固的材料。
蛛网看上去脆弱，实则是自然界最非凡的结构之一。
我们不必喜爱蜘蛛，但应学会宽容它们。
下次在墙角见到一只，不妨放过它——自然界的平衡依赖这些卑微的盟友。
消灭它们会打破历经数百万年才演化而成的生态系统。

en:
On 14 July 1865, Edward Whymper led a party to the summit of the Matterhorn.
It was the last great Alpine peak to be conquered, and the climbers knew the risks.
During the descent, the rope snapped, and four men plunged down the precipice to their deaths.
Whymper alone survived, though the tragedy haunted him for the rest of his life.
Only after the accident did mountaineers pay greater attention to the safety of their equipment.
The Matterhorn, which rises sharply above Zermatt, still draws climbers from around the world.
Fierce as its north face may be, it remains a symbol of both triumph and disaster.
The cord that broke had already shown signs of wear, as Whymper later admitted.
cn:
一八六五年七月十四日，爱德华·Whymper 率领队伍登顶马特霍恩峰。
那是最后一座被征服的阿尔卑斯名峰，登山者深知其中的风险。
下山途中绳索断裂，四人坠入悬崖丧生。
唯有 Whymper 幸存，但这场悲剧终生萦绕着他。
事故之后，登山者才开始更加重视装备安全。
马特霍恩峰陡峭地耸立于采尔马特之上，至今仍吸引世界各地的登山者。
尽管北壁险峻，它仍是胜利与灾难的双重象征。
Whymper 后来承认，断裂的绳索早已出现磨损迹象。

en:
Vera Petrova, who was born blind, developed an extraordinary ability to perceive the world through touch.
Running her fingers over an object, she could identify its shape, size and even its colour.
Scientists, astonished by the accuracy of her descriptions, subjected her to weeks of rigorous tests.
The fact that she could form mental images by touch challenged accepted ideas about human senses.
When sight is lost, other sensory powers may develop to compensate.
Her case, which has been studied for decades, reveals the vast potential of the human brain.
The tests confirmed that her ability was genuine, not the result of guesswork.
Petrova's hands, it seemed, could truly see.
cn:
自幼失明的 Vera Petrova 发展出凭触觉感知世界的非凡能力。
她用手指抚过物体，便能辨认其形状、大小乃至颜色。
科学家对她描述的准确性深感震惊，对她进行了数周的严格测试。
她能凭触觉在脑中形成图像这一事实，挑战了关于人类感官的既有观念。
视力丧失时，其他感官能力可能发展以作补偿。
她的案例已被研究数十年，揭示了人脑的巨大潜力。
测试证实她的能力真实可信，并非猜测的结果。
Petrova 的双手，似乎真的能看见。

en:
The gap between parents and their adolescent children is a subject of endless debate.
Young people, eager to establish their identity, often resent being treated like children.
Parents, who possess the wisdom of experience, may fail to understand the pressures facing youth today.
It is essential that both sides listen to each other rather than merely criticise.
The author suggests that tolerance, not authority, is the key to mutual understanding.
Teenagers should try to respect the views of their elders, while parents ought to give them room to grow.
Resenting the freedom of the young is as harmful as rejecting the counsel of the old.
Only through genuine dialogue can the generation gap be bridged.
cn:
父母与青春期子女之间的鸿沟，是永无休止的争论话题。
年轻人渴望确立自我认同，往往厌恶被当作孩子对待。
拥有阅历的父母，可能无法理解当今青年面临的压力。
双方相互倾听而非一味批评，至关重要。
作者认为，宽容而非权威，才是相互理解的关键。
青少年应尊重长辈的意见，父母也应给予他们成长的空间。
嫉妒年轻人的自由，与拒绝老人的忠告同样有害。
唯有真诚的对话，才能弥合代沟。

en:
The author argues that modern competitive sport has little to do with the virtues it claims to promote.
Spectators, fierce in their loyalty, care more about victory than about skill or fair play.
International contests are often exploited for nationalism rather than friendship.
Under enormous pressure to win, some athletes have resorted to taking dope.
Fierce as the rivalry may be, true sporting spirit demands respect for the opponent.
If sport were truly about character, commercial interests would play a smaller part.
Only by returning to the ideals of fair play can athletics regain its dignity.
Sport should unite people, not incite the hatred that nationalism breeds.
cn:
作者认为，现代竞技体育与其标榜的美德已相去甚远。
忠诚激烈的观众更在乎胜负，而非技艺或公平竞争。
国际赛事常被民族主义利用，而非促进友谊。
在巨大的获胜压力下，一些运动员铤而走险服用兴奋剂。
尽管竞争激烈，真正的体育精神要求尊重对手。
如果体育真正关乎品格，商业利益便不应占这么大比重。
只有回归公平竞争的理想，体育运动才能重获尊严。
体育应团结人心，而非煽动民族主义所滋生的仇恨。

en:
Bats are the only mammals capable of true flight, yet they are among the most misunderstood creatures.
In total darkness, they navigate by echolocation, emitting ultrasonic cries beyond human hearing.
The cries bounce back from obstacles, enabling the bat to judge distance with remarkable precision.
Scientists accept the fact that a single bat may consume half its body weight in insects each night.
Being nocturnal, bats play a vital role in controlling pest populations.
Many species have been driven to the edge of extinction by the destruction of their habitat.
The evidence that bats benefit agriculture is now widely recognised.
Far from being creatures of horror, they are essential guardians of the ecological balance.
cn:
蝙蝠是唯一能真正飞行的哺乳动物，却是最被误解的生物之一。
在完全黑暗中，它们靠回声定位导航，发出人耳听不见的超声尖叫。
声波从障碍物反弹回来，使蝙蝠能以惊人精度判断距离。
科学家接受这一事实：一只蝙蝠一夜可吃掉相当于自身体重一半的昆虫。
蝙蝠夜间活动，在控制害虫数量方面发挥着重要作用。
许多物种因栖息地破坏而被推向灭绝边缘。
蝙蝠有益于农业的证据现已广为认可。
它们绝非恐怖生物，而是生态平衡不可或缺的守护者。

en:
The Trading Standards Department exists to protect consumers from fraud and unsafe goods.
Officers have the power to enter any shop, inspect goods and take specimens for testing.
Regulations which protect the public have been strengthened in recent years.
Goods that fail to meet the required standards must be withdrawn from sale.
Repeat offenders may be prosecuted and fined in court.
A formal complaint lodged by a customer led to the discovery of short-weight scales.
Samples were taken and analysed in the department's own laboratory.
Without such vigilance, shopping would be a far riskier affair for ordinary people.
cn:
贸易标准局的存在，是为了保护消费者免受欺诈和不合格商品的侵害。
执法人员有权进入任何商店、检查商品并取样化验。
近年来，保护公众的规章已得到加强。
不符合要求的商品必须下架停售。
屡犯者可能被起诉并处以法庭罚款。
一位顾客的正式投诉导致缺斤短两的秤被查处。
样品被取样并在该部门自有实验室中分析。
若无此类监管，普通人的日常购物将危险得多。

en:
During the reign of Elizabeth I, England faced threats from within and abroad.
Secret agents, who infiltrated enemy circles, brought back intelligence vital to the Queen's survival.
Letters that conspirators had exchanged in code were intercepted and deciphered in time.
The Queen had already been warned before the plot against her was carried out.
Anthony Bacon, one of the most trusted agents, operated across Europe on her behalf.
Espionage, though distasteful, played a crucial role in preserving the realm.
Agents discovered that several conspirators had been plotting for months.
In an age of intrigue, reliable intelligence was worth more than any army.
cn:
伊丽莎白一世统治时期，英格兰面临内外威胁。
渗入敌营的秘密特工带回了对女王存亡至关重要的情报。
阴谋者用密码往来的信件被及时截获并破译。
针对女王的阴谋实施之前，女王已得到警告。
最受信任的特工之一安东尼·培根代表她在欧洲各地活动。
间谍活动虽不光彩，却在维护王国方面发挥了关键作用。
特工发现数名阴谋者已密谋数月。
在阴谋四伏的时代，可靠的情报比任何军队都更有价值。

en:
Silicon Valley, which stretches south of San Francisco, has become the global centre of technological innovation.
Companies that began in garages have grown into giants that disrupt entire industries.
There is a belief that anyone with a good idea and enough courage can succeed here.
The culture of the Valley encourages entrepreneurs to iterate rapidly and tolerate failure.
Semiconductor research, from which the region takes its name, laid the foundations decades ago.
Young engineers flock to the Valley, drawn by the hope of building something that changes the world.
The fact that so many startups fail does little to dampen the enthusiasm of newcomers.
In this remarkable corner of California, a single idea can still transform human life.
cn:
硅谷绵延于旧金山以南，已成为全球技术创新的中心。
从车库起步的公司已成长为颠覆整个行业的巨头。
有一种信念：任何有好点子和足够勇气的人都能在这里成功。
硅谷文化鼓励创业者快速迭代、容忍失败。
半导体研究——该地区由此得名——在数十年前奠定了根基。
年轻工程师涌入硅谷，希望打造改变世界的产品。
如此多的初创公司失败，却丝毫未减新来者的热情。
在加利福尼亚这片非凡的土地上，一个点子仍能改变人类生活。

en:
Bertrand Russell offers practical advice on how to grow old with dignity and contentment.
Growing old gracefully, he argues, means keeping your interests broad and your mind active.
The elderly should avoid dwelling on the past and resenting the freedom of the young.
Some people withdraw from social life too early, losing the vigour that moderate activity preserves.
I wish more senior citizens would remain engaged with the world around them.
If only they had not allowed regrets to dominate their later years.
Contentment in old age comes not from idleness but from continued participation in life.
Russell's philosophy reminds us that the quality of our years matters more than their number.
cn:
伯特兰·罗素就如何有尊严、满足地老去提出了切实建议。
他认为，优雅地老去意味着保持广泛的兴趣和活跃的思维。
老年人应避免沉溺往事、嫉妒年轻人的自由。
有些人过早退出社交生活，失去了适度活动所能保持的活力。
我希望更多老年人继续投身于周围的世界。
要是他们没有让悔恨主宰晚年就好了。
老年的满足并非来自闲散，而是来自对生活的持续参与。
罗素的哲学提醒我们，生命的质量比年岁长短更为重要。

en:
The relationship between banks and their customers is more complex than it appears on the surface.
People deposit savings in banks, seeking security, yet they are also delegating responsibility for their money.
The formidable architecture of bank buildings is designed to inspire confidence and authority.
The financial sector comprises banks, insurers and investment firms serving different needs.
Too much trust is placed in institutions that depositors barely understand.
Savings accumulated over a lifetime may be lost irrevocably if a bank fails.
The psychology of banking — why people hand over their money so readily — deserves closer study.
Understanding this relationship is essential for anyone who wishes to manage their affairs wisely.
cn:
银行与顾客之间的关系，远比表面看上去复杂。
人们把积蓄存入银行以求保障，同时也在把理财责任委托出去。
银行建筑那令人敬畏的外观，旨在树立信心与权威。
金融部门由银行、保险公司和投资机构组成，服务不同需求。
储户对几乎不了解的机构寄予了过多信任。
一生积累的储蓄若银行倒闭，便可能不可挽回地损失。
银行心理——人们为何如此轻易把钱交出去——值得深入研究。
理解这种关系，对希望明智理财的人至关重要。
